question_text: Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in regulating electrolyte balance, which maintains fluid stability through filtration and reabsorption functions.

Now please segment the organ on the right side of the image that is smaller than the right kidney. Its capsular structure includes the adipose capsule and renal fascia, and it primarily participates in acid-base balance regulation.
Please segment this important organ of the urinary system on the left side of the image, which is involved in body fluid regulation and urine production, and has significant endocrine functions.

Now please segment another urinary system organ on the right side of the image that is smaller than the right kidney. It is primarily responsible for acid-base balance regulation and maintenance of body fluid osmotic pressure.

Please segment a medium-sized organ on the left side of the image. This organ has the function of secreting bile to promote fat digestion, and its pathological changes often manifest as jaundice symptoms.

Now we need to segment an organ located on the left side of the image that is significantly larger than the liver. Its functional unit is the nephron, which forms final urine through filtration and reabsorption, and excretes nitrogenous waste.

Next, please segment the organ on the right side of the image that is smaller than the right kidney. It reabsorbs most of the primary urine through renal tubules. In traditional Chinese medicine theory, it is referred to as the "foundation of innate constitution," primarily responsible for storing essence and governing water.
Please segment the largest gland in the human body on the left side of the image, which is closely associated with jaundice symptoms.
Now please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is larger than the liver in area. It regulates electrolyte balance through glomerular filtration and reabsorption.
Next, segment the paired organ on the right side of the image that has the same renal function as the left kidney but a slightly smaller area.
Please segment the important organ on the left side of the image that is involved in vitamin metabolism, as this organ is closely related to the synthesis and storage of various vitamins.

Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image, which is slightly larger than the liver. It is responsible for filtering blood, maintaining fluid balance, and also has endocrine functions.

Next, please segment the organ on the right side of the image. It is slightly smaller than the right kidney, has similar functions but is located on the opposite side, primarily regulating water and osmotic balance in the body.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is primarily metabolic in function, mainly responsible for deoxidation, glycogen storage, and bile production.

There is a slightly smaller organ on the left side of the liver, located on the same side of the image, which regulates body fluid osmotic pressure through renal tubules and excretes nitrogenous waste.
Compared to the right kidney, there is a smaller symmetrical organ on the right side of the image, whose nephrons collect urine through renal calyces and secrete erythropoietin.
Please segment the major metabolic organ on the left side of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation, as its impaired function may lead to sex hormone imbalance.
Now segment the smaller paired excretory organ on the left side of the image compared to the liver area, whose functional unit consists of renal corpuscles and renal tubules.
Next, segment the smaller symmetrical organ located to the right of the right kidney, which maintains acid-base balance by regulating H+ and HCO3-.
Please segment the largest glandular organ on the left side of the image. This organ plays a crucial detoxification role by transforming toxic substances for excretion from the body.

Now please segment the organ located on the left side of the image, which is significantly smaller in size than the liver. Its parenchyma is divided into cortex and medulla, and the outer capsule provides support and fixation functions.

Next, segment the paired organ on the right side of the image, which is similar in function to the right kidney but slightly larger in area. It maintains fluid homeostasis by regulating water and osmotic balance.
Please segment the important organ on the left side of the image that has vitamin metabolic functions, as it is involved in the synthesis and storage of various vitamins.
Now please segment the much smaller organ on the left side of the image compared to the liver, which is responsible for filtering blood and maintaining fluid balance.

Next, please segment the symmetrical organ on the right side of the image that is slightly larger than the right kidney. It regulates urine formation through glomerular filtration and reabsorption mechanisms.
Please segment the larger organ on the left side of the image, which is an important thermogenic organ and also has hematopoietic function during fetal development.

Now please segment another organ located on the left side of the human body, which is smaller than the liver. Its outer layer consists of an adipose capsule and renal fascia, serving functions of fixation and protection.

Next, segment the organ on the right side of the image. It is slightly larger than the right kidney, with parenchyma composed of cortex and medulla, and it helps maintain stable blood pH by regulating acid-base balance.

Finally, segment the organ in the lower right corner of the image. It is the smallest among the four targets, primarily composed of white pulp and red pulp, and responsible for immune responses to blood-borne antigens.
Please segment the largest metabolic and detoxification organ on the left side of the image, which is responsible for bile secretion and toxic substance transformation.

Now please segment another organ on the left side of the image, which is much smaller than the liver. Its primary functions include excreting nitrogenous waste and regulating blood pressure and erythropoiesis.

Next, segment the organ on the right side of the image. It is slightly larger than the right kidney, has similar functions but is located on the opposite side, and is also involved in urine formation and the regulation of calcium and phosphorus metabolism.

Finally, segment the organ in the lower right corner of the image. It is the smallest of the four targets and is closely related to immune function and blood filtration, adjacent to the stomach and left kidney.
Please segment the important organ on the left side of the image that is involved in vitamin metabolism and hormone inactivation.
A smaller organ is visible below the right side of the liver, located at the center of the image. Its parenchyma is divided into cortex and medulla. Please segment this structure.
There is another similar structure on the left side of the right kidney, approximately twice the size of the right kidney, also located in the center of the image. Please perform segmentation.
There is an organ related to the lymphatic system in the lower right corner of the image, near the gastrosplenic ligament area. Please segment this target.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation, as dysfunction of this organ may lead to abnormal sexual characteristics.
Now please segment the organ in the center of the image, which is much smaller than the liver and accounts for about one-quarter of the body's total blood flow.
Finally, segment the organ in the lower right corner of the image. It is slightly larger than the left kidney and serves as the primary filtration site for circulating blood in the body.

Please segment the larger solid organ on the left side of the image. This organ serves as the primary hematopoietic organ during fetal development, and its function is replaced by bone marrow in adulthood but can resume hematopoietic activity under certain pathological conditions.
Compared to the liver, there is a significantly smaller organ in the central region of the image that regulates electrolyte balance through glomerular filtration and tubular reabsorption.

In the lower right region of the image, there is a lymphatic organ slightly larger than the left kidney. Its parenchyma is divided into white pulp and red pulp, serving as the primary site for specific immunity.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that plays a crucial role in blood regulation and is closely associated with vitamin metabolism.
Compared to the liver, please segment the smaller lymphoid organ located at the lower right side of the image, which functions in blood filtration.

Please segment the larger parenchymal organ on the left side of the image, which serves as the primary hematopoietic organ during fetal development and continues to play a crucial role in coagulation regulation in adults.

Now please segment a structure in the lower right side of the image, which is much smaller than the liver and is the largest lymphoid organ in the human body, normally not palpable on the body surface.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation, as dysfunction of this organ may lead to sex hormone imbalance.
Now please segment the peripheral immune organ located at the lower right of the image, which is significantly smaller in volume than the liver and serves as a crucial site for immune responses.
Please segment the largest gland on the left side of the image that is primarily responsible for metabolic functions and bile secretion.
On the right side of the liver, there is a significantly smaller organ involved in blood filtration with characteristic splenic notches on its surface. Please segment this structure.
Please segment the main metabolic organ in the central region of the image, which is responsible for bile secretion and plays a crucial detoxification role.

There is a significantly smaller lymphoid organ on the right side of the liver, adjacent to the gastrosplenic ligament and possibly accompanied by an accessory spleen structure. Please segment this target.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is primarily responsible for metabolic functions and bile production.
Compared to the liver, there is a significantly smaller immune-related organ on the right side of the image. Please segment this structure.

Please segment the abdominal organ adjacent to the stomach on the left side of the image. This organ exhibits positional changes during respiration and is often palpable below the costal margin during pediatric physical examinations.
Please locate and segment the organ on the left side of the image that is internally and externally connected to the gallbladder and has hematopoietic function during fetal development.
Please locate and segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in regulating body water metabolism and moves with respiration.

Please segment an important organ of the urinary system displayed on the left side of the image. In traditional Chinese medicine theory, this organ is regarded as the foundation of innate constitution, with functions of storing essence and governing water. Its functional units consist of renal corpuscles and renal tubules, and the urine produced is ultimately discharged into the minor calyx through the papillary ducts of the kidney.

Please locate and segment the organ on the left side of the image that is closely related to excretory function and is referred to as the "congenital foundation" in traditional Chinese medicine theory.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that regulates fluid homeostasis and electrolyte balance.

Now please segment the organ in the central region of the image that is larger than the right kidney. This organ plays an important role in acid-base balance regulation and reabsorption of primary urine.
Please segment the largest metabolic organ on the left side of the image, which is responsible for bile secretion and protein synthesis.
Now segment the left organ located below the liver, which is smaller than the liver and involved in acid-base balance regulation.
Next, segment the organ in the center of the image, which is smaller than the right kidney and primarily functions to regulate water-electrolyte balance.
Finally, segment the lymphatic organ on the right side of the image, whose area lies between the two kidneys and serves immune response functions.
Please segment the important organ on the left side of the image that is responsible for vitamin metabolism and bile secretion.
Now please segment one of the paired organs located in the center of the image, which is smaller than the liver but functions in blood pressure regulation and urine production.
Next, segment another organ that is also part of the urinary system. It is located at the center of the image and is smaller than the right kidney, with the function of regulating acid-base balance.
Finally, segment the lymphatic organ on the right side of the image, which is slightly larger than the left kidney and functions in blood storage and immune response.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation. Impairment of this organ's function may lead to sex hormone imbalance.

In the right side of the liver, near the center of the image, there is a relatively small organ that receives approximately 1/4 to 1/5 of the total blood flow in the body. Its primary functions are blood filtration and acid-base balance regulation.
In a position symmetrical to the right kidney, there is a smaller organ with parenchyma divided into cortex and medulla, possessing both urinary and endocrine functions.

On the right side of the image, there is an organ located between the liver and the right kidney. It is the largest lymphatic organ in the human body and filters approximately 90% of the circulating blood.
Please segment the largest metabolic organ on the left side of the image, which is involved in bile secretion and protein synthesis.
Now segment the organ located at the center of the image, which is much smaller than the liver and primarily responsible for regulating fluid balance and filtering blood.
Finally, segment the organ on the right side of the image, which is larger than the right kidney but smaller than the liver, as it is an important site for immune responses.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image involved in hormone inactivation, as its impaired function may lead to sex hormone imbalance.
Now segment the smaller lymphoid organ on the right side of the image compared to the liver, where B cells account for approximately 60% of the total lymphocyte population.
Please segment the largest glandular organ on the left side of the image, which has hematopoietic function during fetal development.

Now please segment the organ located on the right side of the image. It is smaller than the liver and is the largest lymphoid organ in the human body, primarily composed of sinusoids with splenic notches on its surface.
Please segment the important organ on the left side of the image that is involved in the production of clotting factors and hormone inactivation.
Compared to the liver, there is a smaller lymphoid-rich organ located centrally in the image. Please segment this structure.

Please segment the largest gland on the left side of the image. Although this organ is not a primary hematopoietic organ in adulthood, it can resume hematopoietic function under certain pathological conditions.

Another organ is visible below the right side of the liver, with a significantly smaller area than the liver. It serves as the primary filtration site for circulating blood in the body, purifying approximately 90% of the blood.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is primarily responsible for metabolic functions and bile production.
There is a significantly smaller peripheral immune organ located inferior to the right lobe of the liver. Please segment this structure.
Please segment the large organ in the central region of the image that has vitamin metabolism and detoxification functions.

On the right side of the image, there is a significantly smaller organ located inferior to the liver. Please segment this immune-related organ, whose structure includes the marginal zone and splenic hilum.

Please segment the organ on the left side of the image. This organ forms the initial urine through glomerular filtration, and most of the initial urine is reabsorbed as it passes through the renal tubules. Its capsule consists of three layers from outer to inner: the renal fascia, adipose capsule, and fibrous capsule.

Now please segment the organ in the center of the image. This organ is larger than the right kidney, with its parenchyma divided into an outer cortex and an inner medulla. The cortex is composed of glomeruli and renal tubules.
Please segment the largest glandular organ in the central region of the image, which plays a key regulatory role in coagulation function.
Now please segment the lymphatic organ located on the right side of the image, which is significantly smaller in volume than the liver and prone to damage under external force.
Please segment the parenchymal organ in the central region of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation, which also has hematopoietic function during the fetal period.
Now segment the lymphatic organ located on the right side of the image, which is significantly smaller in volume than the liver and serves as the primary filtration site for circulating blood.

Please segment the main metabolic organ in the central region of the image. This organ can move with respiration and should not extend beyond the lower edge of the costal arch in normal adults. It also has important functions in bile secretion and glycogen storage.

Please segment the main metabolic organ in the central region of the image. This organ is responsible for energy metabolism regulation and has the function of secreting digestive juices. The digestive juices it produces are stored and transported through a specific duct system.

Please segment the important organ in the central region of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation. Dysfunction of this organ can lead to sex hormone imbalance and it participates in regulating water metabolism and acid-base balance.
Please segment the main metabolic organ in the central region of the image, which often exhibits functional abnormalities in various poisoning conditions.

Please segment the organ in the central region of the image that is involved in hormone inactivation. In traditional Chinese medicine theory, this organ is internally and externally related to the gallbladder, primarily stores blood, and opens into the eyes. Long-term impairment of its function may lead to the loss of gender-characteristic hair.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is responsible for filtering blood impurities, maintaining fluid balance, and has endocrine functions in the urinary system.
Now please segment another organ of the urinary system located in the center of the image, which is larger than the right kidney. It also has endocrine functions and promotes erythropoiesis.

Please segment the endocrine organ on the left side of the image that is involved in regulating fluid balance and urine production. In traditional Chinese medicine theory, it is closely related to bone, marrow, and auditory functions.
Now please segment another organ of the same type located at the center of the image, which is slightly larger than the right kidney and typically positioned slightly higher anatomically.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is involved in water metabolism and acid-base balance regulation, and is internally and externally related to the gallbladder.
Now we need to segment another organ located on the left side of the image that is significantly larger than the liver, which is responsible for filtering blood and maintaining electrolyte balance.

Next, please segment the organ in the central region of the image. It is slightly larger than the right kidney and has similar functions but primarily focuses on regulating osmotic balance of body fluids.
Please segment the largest metabolic organ on the left side of the image, which is primarily responsible for detoxification and bile production.
Now segment the organ located on the left side of the liver, which is slightly larger than the liver and primarily functions to regulate acid-base balance.

Next, segment the organ in the central region of the image. It is larger than the right kidney and serves as the functional unit for urine production, containing renal corpuscles and collecting ducts.

Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that is internally and externally connected to the gallbladder and primarily stores blood. This organ plays a significant role in energy metabolism.

There is a slightly smaller organ on the left side of the liver, which belongs to the urinary system and has endocrine functions. Traditional Chinese medicine believes it governs the storage of essence and controls water. Please segment this organ.

In the center of the image, there is a paired organ slightly larger than the right kidney, receiving over one-quarter of the body's total blood flow, with a capsular structure consisting of renal fascia and adipose capsule. Please segment this structure.
Please segment the organ on the left side of the image that has detoxification function and participates in water metabolism regulation.
Now please segment another smaller organ located on the same side (left side) of the liver, which is composed of nephrons and involved in electrolyte balance.
Finally, segment the paired organ in the central region of the image. The cortex of this organ contains glomeruli and its area is slightly larger than the previously segmented right counterpart.
Please segment the major metabolic organ on the left side of the image that is responsible for vitamin metabolism, which synthesizes and stores various vitamins as well as produces bile.
Now please segment another important organ located on the left side of the image that is smaller than the liver. It maintains homeostasis by filtering blood and regulating electrolyte balance.
Next, please segment the organ in the center of the image, which is slightly smaller than the right kidney and forms the final urine through glomerular filtration and reabsorption.
Finally, segment the organ on the right side of the image. It is the largest lymphatic organ in the human body, smaller than the left kidney, and functions in both blood storage and immune response.
